problems.title: Dictionary Basics — Creation & Printing
problems.statement: Create a dictionary named marks mapping student names to their marks: Hari: 88, Keshava: 95, Madhava: 76, Yadava: 82, Damodara: 91. Then print the marks of Keshava, and print the marks of Yadava.
constraints: The values above are fixed so your output can be checked exactly.

problems.title: Iterate Over Dictionary Pairs
problems.statement: Given the dictionary {"Raghava": 98, "Madhava": 99, "Keshava": 44}, iterate over its key-value pairs one by one and print each in the exact format name---->marks (four dashes, then a greater-than sign).
constraints: Print pairs in the order they appear in the dictionary.
problems.starterCode:
marks = {"Raghava": 98, "Madhava": 99, "Keshava": 44}
# loop over marks.items() and print each as name---->marks

problems.title: Most Frequent Word (with a Dictionary)
problems.statement: Given a sentence, print the word that occurs the most. Use str1.split() and a dictionary to count occurrences. You can assume there's a single clear winner.
constraints: Words are lowercase, separated by single spaces.
problems.starterCode:
str1 = input()
# build a word -> count dictionary, then print the most frequent word

problems.title: Bird Sanctuary
problems.statement: Badrinarayana visits a bird sanctuary and spots many different birds. The input is a single line listing the birds he sighted, separated by commas (entries may have extra spaces around them). Print the most sighted bird and its count, separated by a space.
constraints: Strip extra whitespace (and any trailing punctuation) from each entry before counting.
problems.starterCode:
line = input()
tokens = [t.strip().rstrip(".") for t in line.split(",")]
# count each bird and print "bird count" for the most sighted one
